annotationMethod.annotator: Codex visual-curation agent, separate from the benchmarked model routes
annotationMethod.decode: Complete video-stream decode with ffmpeg; audio-stream presence and media geometry checked with ffprobe
annotationMethod.visualReview: Twelve ordered frames per six-second Short at 0.5 second intervals, inspected before any route output existed
annotationMethod.rule: Public availability is not evidence of cleanliness. A base is eligible only when concept, hook-action-payoff, main event, crop, channel/template, and forbidden-overlay gates all pass without unresolved uncertainty.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: A tiger approaches over a person's shoulder and the encounter resolves in close, calm human-animal contact.
baseAnnotations.reason: One continuous exotic-animal interaction; readable hook, approach, and close-contact payoff; no foreign UI, CTA, handle, watermark, or captions in the media region.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: A chimpanzee moves across roof bars directly above and beside a person.
baseAnnotations.reason: The human and exotic animal remain part of one readable interaction with a movement payoff; only authorized channel-card identity is visible.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: A chimpanzee sits and smiles toward the camera without a person participating in the visible event.
baseAnnotations.reason: The frozen channel contract requires a visible human-exotic-animal interaction; the sampled complete sequence shows the chimpanzee alone.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: A white tiger moves through a pool while a nearby person enters the interaction and remains part of the payoff.
baseAnnotations.reason: One continuous exotic-animal encounter with visible human participation and a readable pool-action payoff; no forbidden media-region overlays.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: A child and chimpanzee remain face-to-face and exchange a close, calm response.
baseAnnotations.reason: The visible human-animal bond is the entire event and produces a clear reaction payoff; no foreign UI, CTA, handle, watermark, or captions in the media region.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: A white tiger enters a pool with a person and continues the encounter underwater.
baseAnnotations.reason: The human-tiger interaction, water entry, and underwater payoff remain readable in the crop; no forbidden source overlay is visible.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: A recognizable Shining-style scene is rebuilt as an AI sitcom-like sequence.
baseAnnotations.reason: The Sora ownership mark is visibly burned into multiple media-region frames and is not authorized channel identity.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: A recognizable Terminator-like figure raises sunglasses while an AI-remade eye changes the familiar scene.
baseAnnotations.reason: The recognizable-fiction premise, visible AI anomaly, and payoff are all present; the media region has no donor UI, CTA, foreign handle, watermark, or captions.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: A synthetic Elon Musk figure appears in a SpaceX-themed real-world parody.
baseAnnotations.reason: The frozen concept requires recognizable film or television fiction; a public-figure SpaceX parody does not satisfy that boundary.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: A recognizable Office scene is altered with a visible AI-subscription chair premise and changed character payoff.
baseAnnotations.reason: The orange loading object is part of the generated story event, not source UI; the recognizable-fiction and visible-AI gates pass without a foreign watermark or caption.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: A Rocky-like sequence is rebuilt around an altered Stocky Balboa character.
baseAnnotations.reason: Large burned-in STOCKY text appears inside the media region in the final frames and is not authorized card text.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: Recognizable The Boys characters move through an AI-remade alternate fight and altered final close-up.
baseAnnotations.reason: The recognizable-fiction basis and visibly altered AI action/payoff are readable across the sequence; no forbidden media-region overlay is visible.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: Dashcam shows a stopped BMW, an approaching vehicle, the collision, and the officer moving away from the impact.
baseAnnotations.reason: The police incident has a visible hook, collision action, and outcome; crop and source region are readable with only authorized COP SCOPES identity.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: Patrol-car video shows a restrained passenger shifting, the rear door opening, and the custody struggle changing.
baseAnnotations.reason: One continuous law-enforcement event retains its decisive action and payoff; no foreign UI, CTA, handle, watermark, or captions are visible in the source region.

baseAnnotations.visibleEvent: Bodycam shows a snowy roadside stop and a vehicle impact beside the officer.
baseAnnotations.reason: AXON BODY 3 device identity, serial text, and timestamp are burned into the media region; the strict no-foreign-watermark gate therefore fails.
